questions.question: What is a page fault?
answers: A physical hardware failure inside RAM modules
A data anomaly where two files share the same sector
A crash caused by syntax compile errors
An interrupt triggered when a program accesses a page not currently mapped in RAM

questions.question: What is the role of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)?
answers: To act as a specialized cache for virtual-to-physical address translations
To handle network data package buffering streams
To map file allocation records sequentially on disk
To store execution metrics logs for background tasks

questions.question: What is the function of the Memory Management Unit (MMU)?
answers: To allocate network sockets to background daemons
To partition hard drive sectors dynamically at runtime
To map virtual addresses generated by the CPU to physical memory locations
To clear cache registers when a context switch occurs

questions.question: What privilege layer does the operating system kernel run in on modern CPUs?
answers: Hypervisor mode
Safe mode
Kernel mode (Ring 0)
User mode (Ring 3)

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a system call?
answers: To compile source code files into machine code
To provide an interface for user programs to request services from the kernel
To clear CPU cache configurations automatically
To manage graphical window rendering pipelines

questions.question: What characterizes a monolithic kernel architecture?
answers: It runs exclusively on virtual machines
All operating system services run inside a single privileged kernel space
OS services are moved to separate user-space modules
It eliminates context switching overhead entirely

questions.question: What is a binary semaphore commonly called when used strictly as a locking mechanism?
answers: Critical Section
Mutex
Condition Variable
Monitor

questions.question: What classical algorithm can be utilized to safely avoid deadlocks in resource allocation?
answers: Round Robin Algorithm
Banker's Algorithm
De Morgan's Optimization
Dijkstra's Shortest Path

questions.question: What condition occurs when multiple threads modify shared data concurrently with output depending on execution order?
answers: Livelock loop
Race Condition
Deadlock state
Starvation anomaly

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the Coffman conditions required for a deadlock?
answers: Mutual Exclusion
Circular Wait
Preemption
Hold and Wait
